title: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important)

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.2(1) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.2(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.31: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.31' running on nis2_entity_classification.csv, CMDB export (index=cmdb or lookup), ServiceNow company records, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `nis2_entity_classification.csv`, CMDB export (`index=cmdb` or lookup), ServiceNow company records
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
| join legal_entity_id [
    search index=cmdb earliest=-1d
    | stats values(nis2_entity_tag) as cmdb_tags by legal_entity_id
]
| eval mismatch=if(NOT match(mvjoin(cmdb_tags,"|"), entity_tier), 1, 0)
| where mismatch=1 OR isnull(cmdb_tags)
| table legal_entity_id, entity_tier, cmdb_tags, mismatch
description: Incorrect classification drives wrong reporting deadlines and control expectations. This use case reconciles legal entity classification in `nis2_entity_classification.csv` with CMDB ownership, revenue sector tags, and national registry identifiers — flagging assets attached to misclassified entities.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cmdb. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Establish authoritative classification decisions in legal function; (2) push classification to CMDB nightly; (3) alert enterprise architecture on CMDB vs register mismatches; (4) re-run after M&A events; (5) document appeals and reclassification decisions. Indexes required: `index=cmdb`; Lookups: `nis2_entity_classification`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv`.
visualization: Table (mismatches), Single value (entities needing review), Sankey (entity to asset counts).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `nis2_entity_classification.csv`, CMDB export (`index=cmdb` or lookup), ServiceNow company records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Establish authoritative classification decisions in legal function; (2) push classification to CMDB nightly; (3) alert enterprise architecture on CMDB vs register mismatches; (4) re-run after M&A events; (5) document appeals and reclassification decisions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
| join legal_entity_id [
    search index=cmdb earliest=-1d
    | stats values(nis2_entity_tag) as cmdb_tags by legal_entity_id
]
| eval mismatch=if(NOT match(mvjoin(cmdb_tags,"|"), entity_tier), 1, 0)
| where mismatch=1 OR isnull(cmdb_tags)
| table legal_entity_id, entity_tier, cmdb_tags, mismatch
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important) (Art. 2(1), national transposition)** — Incorrect classification drives wrong reporting deadlines and control expectations. This use case reconciles legal entity classification in `nis2_entity_classification.csv` with CMDB ownership, revenue sector tags, and national registry identifiers — flagging assets attached to misclassified entities.

Documented **Data sources**: `nis2_entity_classification.csv`, CMDB export (`index=cmdb` or lookup), ServiceNow company records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mismatch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where mismatch=1 OR isnull(cmdb_tags)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: cmdb_tags, entity_tier, legal_entity_id, mismatch.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (mismatches), Single value (entities needing review), Sankey (entity to asset counts).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for incorrect classification drives wrong reporting deadlines and control expectations. this use case reconciles legal entity classification in `nis2_entity_classification.csv` with cmdb ownership, rev. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
